name: Sara Lee's Carrot Square Cake
recipeIngredient: 1 tablespoon Light corn syrup or pancake syrup
recipeInstructions:
1. In a large bowl, combine the eggs, vanilla extract, oil, salt, baking powder, cinnamon, sugar, all-purpose flour, grated carrots, and chopped walnuts. Mix with an electric mixer on medium-high speed for about 3 minutes, scraping the sides of the bowl often.
2. Remove the beaters and stir in the remaining ingredients until combined.
3. Grease and flour a 9-inch square pan. Spread the batter evenly in the pan.
4. Bake at 325°F (165°C) for about 50 minutes. Cool in the pan for approximately 30 minutes.
5. Frost the cooled cake with Cream Cheese Frosting and sprinkle additional chopped walnuts on top.
Cream Cheese Frosting:
1. Beat the cream cheese and butter together until light and fluffy using a medium-high speed electric mixer.
2. Gradually add half of the powdered sugar, increasing the speed to high, while continuing to beat for about 1 minute.
3. Add the orange extract and spice island orange peel, then continue beating until smooth.
4. Scrape down the sides of the bowl often.
5. Resume beating in the remaining powdered sugar until well combined.
6. Frost the sides and top of the cake before serving.
